I am writing to express my disappointment with my recent experience at this establishment. I visited for the first time and had high expectations, but unfortunately, my visit fell short. We arrived at 9:20 PM and were initially given the option to dine in, despite being told we could do so.

However, after requesting to dine in despite the online and physical signage clearly stating that the restaurant was open until 10:00 PM, we were informed that dine-in service was no longer available after 9:30 PM by the staff member who took our order. This information was not provided prior to us being seated, and as a result, we had already made the decision to dine in.

Furthermore, allowing other parties to dine in after us shortly after contradicted the information we were given. This treatment was not only confusing but also came across as inconsistent.

Additionally, when I returned to collect my food, I noticed that the sauce I had ordered was missing. Upon returning to the restaurant, I was assured by the staff member that the sauce was indeed available, but upon returning home, I found that it was indeed missing. This oversight not only resulted in my having to wait for my complete order but also potentially could have led to me paying extra for a sauce that was never provided

We recently tried a chicken sandwich at Crimson Chicken and unfortunately had a disappointing experience. Initially, we were on a chicken tour and stopped by Raising Cane's first but decided to try Crimson Chicken due to their enticing social media advertising. While their chicken looked great, the taste left much to be desired. The sandwich was dry, overly seasoned, and lacked moisture despite the standard toppings. We opted for the Crimson Sandwich, priced at $12.23 after tax.

Shortly after eating, both my wife and I started feeling unwell. I had a persistent scratchy sensation in my throat for an hour, while my wife experienced stomach discomfort, as did I. Reflecting on it, we suspect the sandwich was excessively salty due to over-seasoning. The wait time was short, only 5-7 minutes, but it seemed like there was a lack of care in preparation. The bathroom was dirty, which was also concerning.

Initially, the customer service was okay, but when we called to report our issue, it took a turn for the worse. My wife was hung up on twice before finally speaking with a manager, who is also the owner. Unfortunately, the response was equally dismissive and rude. We were shocked by the lack of concern for food and customer safety.

We don't plan on returning due to the poor food quality and terrible customer service. We hope this review helps others make an informed decision and prevents someone from having a similar experience.

Absolutely phenomenal! Crimson Coward isn't just a Nashville hot chicken restaurant; it's an experience from start to finish, earning every single one of these five stars.
From the moment you walk in, the atmosphere sets the perfect tone. It's vibrant, energetic, and incredibly inviting, with a cool, modern twist on classic hot chicken joint vibes. You feel right at home, whether you're grabbing a quick bite or settling in for a satisfying meal.
But what truly elevates Crimson Coward is the staff. Every single person we encountered was genuinely friendly, welcoming, and incredibly efficient. They were happy to answer questions about the heat levels (which, trust me, are no joke!) and made excellent recommendations. Their passion for the food and commitment to excellent service truly shines through, making the entire dining experience even more enjoyable.
And then there's the food – oh, the food! Delicious doesn't even begin to cover it. The Nashville hot chicken is a masterpiece. Crispy on the outside, incredibly juicy and tender on the inside, and packed with flavor that goes beyond just heat. We tried a few different spice levels, and each one was perfectly balanced, allowing the incredible chicken to be the star. The sides are just as impressive, from the creamy mac and cheese to the tangy coleslaw, each complementing the chicken perfectly. You can tell they use high-quality, fresh ingredients, and it makes all the difference.
Crimson Coward is a must-visit for any hot chicken lover, or really, anyone looking for a truly delicious and memorable meal. This place is a gem, and we'll definitely be back for more!

Outstanding Experience at Crimson Coward! Fredericksburg, VA

Crimson Coward offers five heat levels for their Nashville hot chicken, ranging from Country (no heat) to Mild, Medium, Hot (feel the burn), and the extreme BURRRN, baby burn for true heat seekers, with each level providing progressively more intense spice while retaining flavor, notes Crimson Coward and the hoofprint – Walnut High School.

Country: Full of flavor, zero heat, great for kids or spice-averse diners, say Crimson Coward and the hoofprint – Walnut High School.  Mild: A subtle touch of heat, a hint of spice, according to Crimson Coward and the hoofprint – Walnut High School.  Medium: A noticeable "slap of heat," perfect for those wanting flavor with a kick, note Crimson Coward and the hoofprint – Walnut High School.  Hot: Where you start to "feel the burn," according to Crimson Coward and the hoofprint – Walnut High School.  BURRRN, baby burn: The highest level, extremely spicy, designed for daredevils, state Crimson Coward and the hoofprint – Walnut High School.

UPDATED January 2026:
We recently ordered from Vocelli Pizza via UberEats.  The Uber Eats site is poorly set up for this pizza place, as there’s no way to modify specialty pizzas (I want extra sauce) or create your own pizza and make half toppings on the pizza – this can be changed by the restaurant, they just need to do it.  We ordered their Quatro Cheese and an order of their pepperoni rolls.

The food was delivered quickly and was still a decent, warm temperature when it arrived, which is how we prefer pizza.  We want it as hot and fresh as possible.  This pizza tasted GREAT!  Very very good!  Regarding the extra sauce I wanted, I called the restaurant after placing my UberEats order and asked them if they would add extra sauce to our order and the young man on the phone said he would… and he did!  It was perfectly made, with a nice amount of their tasty sauce on it.  The pepperoni rolls are legitimately some of the best I’ve had.  I’ve been to many places in Pittsburgh, where they sell them everywhere and where Vocellis was founded and the pepperoni rolls I get from my two local Vocellis are just as good as any I’ve had up there.

Definitely will order from this location again!

______________________________________________________________
It had been a couple of years since I’ve had Vocelli’s so we used Uber Eats to give them a try recently.  They had a promotion of free peperoni rolls with a large pizza, so we got a large peperoni pizza and those pepperoni rolls.

The pizza was great!  Very tasty, especially the next day after reheating it.  The crust was spot on, flavorful, and tender!  The little peperoni they use cups up nicely too and the cheese was great.  I just wish it had a little more tomato sauce on it.  I opted for a side of the garlic sauce to go with it as a dip and that sauce was awesome!  Great for dipping the crust into!!

The pepperoni rolls were so good and very soft, tender, and not dry or hard.  Dipping them into a side of their tomato sauce really finished them off perfectly!

Hope I don’t wait another year or more to try their pizza again… This was pretty good!

Vocelli is a fantastic restaurant that offers a delectable selection of pizza and subs, accompanied by outstanding service from their attentive staff. During my recent visit, I had the pleasure of indulging in their menu and experiencing their top-notch customer service.

Let's start with the food. The pizzas at Vocelli are absolutely mouthwatering. The crust is perfectly baked, with a delightful balance of crispiness and chewiness. The sauce is rich and flavorful, complemented by a generous amount of gooey, melted cheese. I particularly enjoyed the wide variety of toppings available, ensuring that there's something to suit everyone's taste buds. Whether you prefer classic combinations like pepperoni and mushrooms or more adventurous options like barbecue chicken, Vocelli has it all. The quality of the ingredients shines through, making each bite a delightful explosion of flavors. The subs are equally impressive, with a wide array of fillings, fresh bread, and the perfect blend of textures.

However, what truly sets Vocelli apart is their exceptional staff service. From the moment I walked in, I was greeted with warm smiles and a genuine willingness to make my dining experience enjoyable. The staff members were knowledgeable about the menu and took the time to patiently answer my questions. They were attentive without being intrusive, ensuring that my needs were met without delay. Despite the restaurant being busy, the staff remained composed and professional, making sure every customer received the attention they deserved. This level of customer care made me feel valued as a guest and left a lasting positive impression.

In terms of the ambiance, Vocelli creates a cozy and inviting atmosphere. The restaurant is clean, well-maintained, and exudes a friendly vibe. Whether you're dining in or ordering for takeout, the staff goes above and beyond to ensure a seamless experience.
